hypothesis: Si-based alloy anodes whose crystal frameworks contain a continuous, percolating sublattice comprising at least 25 at.% transition-metal elements with bulk moduli >100 GPa will exhibit <15% variation in Li+ migration channel radius between fully lithiated and fully delithiated states (measured by DFT-relaxed structures and CAVD) and consequently retain >85% of their initial specific capacity after 200 charge–discharge cycles at C/5.
